mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output that drives corporate earnings revisions upward, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO month-over-month changes is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency is less than 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above restrictive territory reduce equity valuations by increasing the risk-free rate and borrowing costs, triggering negative S&P 500 returns when rates exceed 5 percent.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth concurrent with elevated consumer price inflation signals demand-driven overheating that forces the central bank to intensify restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns as real rates rise.
failure_condition: The interaction of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL changes fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over univariate models (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, declines in real retail sales signal collapsing consumer demand that precedes earnings downgrades and inventory liquidation, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions identified by declining GDP growth, negative month-over-month RSAFS changes fail to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a hit rate above 55%, or Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the unemployment rate reflect deteriorating labor income and falling household confidence, which precede sharp contractions in discretionary spending and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in over 50% of occurrences, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices concurrent with elevated policy rates force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive financial conditions, squeezing corporate margins and multiples to produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction of month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels fails to predict negative 5-day returns with Rank IC above 0.04, or the signal underperforms a univariate FEDFUNDS model in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and wage income that supports top-line revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth exhibits a Rank IC below 0.03 for predicting 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production relative to recent trend signals capacity utilization pressures and operating leverage that translate into margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a walk-forward hit rate above 54%, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
